title: Trichochloa rigida
taxonomy: Trichochloa rigida is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Trichochloa and is considered a member of the order Poales. The classification of Trichochloa rigida is further detailed into the family level as Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. At the rank of order, Trichochloa rigida is a part of Poales. The species is a small, perennial grass.

intro: Trichochloa rigida is a perennial grass species commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, known for its unique rigid leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Trichochloa rigida contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Trichochloa rigida is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tonics, which are commonly consumed for their perceived health benefits.
